name: Parsa Faraji Alamouti
title: Software Engineer
tagline: EECS @ UC Berkeley | ML Researcher | Building innovative solutions with AI and data science
bio: Currently pursuing a Bachelor's degree in Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences at UC Berkeley, with a strong foundation in Mathematics and Computer Science from Berkeley City College. Recognized as Salutatorian of the graduating class of 2025 and a recipient of multiple academic honors, reflecting a commitment to excellence and leadership in STEM. As a Machine Learning Researcher at TokenWorks LLC, contributing to knowledge graph advancements, OCR preprocessing pipeline improvements, and data annotation quality for underrepresented domains. Dedicated to leveraging technical expertise and innovative solutions to address complex challenges in AI and data science.

experience.description: Contributing to knowledge graph and ontology research to improve entity linking and semantic understanding. Designing and testing OCR preprocessing pipelines (deskewing, denoising, contrast enhancement, reading order). Assisting with data annotation and quality evaluation to ensure accuracy in underrepresented domains.
Leading frontend development teams on semester-long client projects. Managing Berkeley student teams building scoped MVPs, internal tools, and prototypes for product teams.
Contributing to educational initiatives and technical curriculum development at UC Berkeley's premier coding organization.

Conducted market and user research to identify trends, customer needs, and growth opportunities. Analyzed content, product performance, and engagement data to guide creative strategies. Produced insights and recommendations that shaped storytelling, branding, and product positioning.
Completed training in Front-End Development, Version Control platforms, and pair programming. Contributed to building donation platform interfaces.

education.degree: B.S. Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences
A.S. Mathematics and Computer Science (4.0 GPA, Salutatorian)
